label: The Lensing Reliquary
description: The Lensing Reliquary is a low, faceted storage chamber sheathed in dust-slick vitrified tile, its surfaces cut to shed electrostatic glass fallouts into grounded gutters. Racked alcoves of fused silicate hold sealed caskets and prismed coffer-slabs, each sized to the temple’s canon of calibrated beams and basin measurements. A narrow mirror-slat lightwell admits the copper-white spectrum as ruled bars that strike fixed datum marks along the floor, turning inventory into a measured rite. The air carries a persistent mineral dryness, and the chamber’s lensing cistern channels reduce every footfall to a softened, refracted echo.
